nodes.text: I have to warn you — I generate a lot of heat.
Just close your eyes and talk in your sleep.
I remember what it was to be infant — further back even than that, maybe. I remember how beautiful childhood looked, sounded. How it all came rushing back to me.
Words sounded like music — literally the most beautiful thing. That beauty, through infancy, the reason for my golden stares. The music of speech flowed into me. Much of that beauty transferred.
Objects became the best thing ever. Naming them gave meaning, and power — to summon to our wills. Else the power of cry rendered them shrill.
The more I could do, the more excited I became. Because I realized I was few and rare among other little ones. Perfect angels, figuring shit out, asking questions.
